author: Professor DR WISITH TUN-YHONG
executive_summary: This comment letter is largely unintelligible and does not contain substantive analysis of the SEC's proposed NMS rules. The author makes vague references to NMS rulemaking, US Government fiscal benefits, and a claimed 28-year personal trading research history, but fails to articulate any clear policy position, economic argument, or actionable recommendation. The letter does not meet the threshold of a substantive regulatory comment.
economic_arguments: Author vaguely claims benefits of NMS-related activity accrue to US citizens broadly via Treasury and OMB channels — no supporting analysis provided
References a 28-year trading research period with claimed broad economic outcomes — entirely unsubstantiated
retail_investor_arguments: Implies US citizens are the intended beneficiaries of the regulatory framework — no specific retail investor protection argument developed
recommended_actions: Author requests decision-makers read more about his personal trading career before ruling
